title: oxalis articulata subsp. floribunda
intro: Oxalis articulata subsp. floribunda, also known as the Purple Wood Sorrel, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Oxalis genus. It is a perennial herb native to South Africa, characterized by its purple flowers and edible, tart leaves. The medicinal properties of Oxalis articulata subsp. floribunda include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant's leaves and flowers can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, offering health benefits like reducing inflammation and improving digestion.
